title: 2022 Huddersfield Giants season
wikitext:
{{short description|English rugby league season}} {{Infobox rugby league team season | name = 2022 Huddersfield Giants Season | year = 2022 | team_colour = maroon; border: solid orange 2px | font_colour = white | league = Super League | league_link = [[Super League XXVII]] | league_rank = 3rd | league_wins = | league_draws = | league_losses = | points_for = | points_against = | playoff_result = Eliminators | cup = Challenge Cup | cup_result = Runner up | cup_wins = | cup_losses = | CEO_title = Chairman | CEO = [[Ken Davy]] | coach_title = Head Coach | coach = [[Ian Watson (rugby league)|Ian Watson]] | captain = [[Aiden Sezer]] | stadium = [[John Smiths Stadium]]
[[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire]] | agg_attendance = | avg_attendance = | high_attendance = | low_attendance = | top_tries = | top_goals = | top_points = | prev_season = [[2021 Huddersfield Giants season|2021]] | next_season = [[2023 Huddersfield Giants season|2023]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Huddersfield Giants seasons |pattern_la1= |pattern_b1= |pattern_ra1= |pattern_sh1 = |pattern_so1 = |leftarm1= |body1 = |rightarm1 = |shorts1 = |socks1 = |pattern_la2 = |pattern_b2= |pattern_ra2= |pattern_sh2 = |pattern_so2 = |leftarm2= |body2= |rightarm2= |shorts2= |socks2= }} In the 2022 rugby league season the [[Huddersfield Giants]] competed in [[Super League XXVII]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. They were coached by [[Ian Watson (rugby league)|Ian Watson]]. ==Season review== On 12 February, Huddersfield began their Super League campaign with a 42–14 away win at [[Toulouse Olympique]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} which they followed with a 26–12 win over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} before suffering their first loss of the season at [[Wigan Warriors]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36688/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League round-up|publisher=RFL |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} At the end of March, Huddersfield progressed from the sixth round of the [[Challenge Cup]] with a 30–16 win at [[Barrow Raiders]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36891/betfred-challenge-cup-round-6-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round 6 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=27 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and then defeated [[Hull F.C.]] in the quarter-finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37006/betfred-challenge-cup-quarter-final-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup quarter-final round-up |publisher=RFL |date=10 April 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} In May, a 25–4 win over Hull KR in the semi-finals saw Huddersfield reach the Challenge Cup final for the first time since 2009.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37227/huddersfield-sweep-aside-hull-kr-to-secure-first-final-appearance-since-2009 |title=Huddersfield sweep aside Hull KR to secure first Final appearance since 2009 |publisher=RFL |date=7 May 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} The final was played at the [[Tottenham Hotspur Stadium]] where they lost 16–14 to Wigan.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60155/wigan-warriors-win-betfred-challenge-cup |title=Wigan Warriors win Betfred Challenge Cup|publisher=RFL |date=28 May 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} Meanwhile their good league form continued and included a run of six wins to the end of June.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60354/betfred-super-league-preview-%7C-round-16 |title=Betfred Super League Preview {{!}} Round 16 |publisher=RFL |date=23 June 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60378/betfred-super-league-round-16-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 16 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=26 June 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} On 18 August, Huddersfield secured their place in the play-offs with a 36–10 win over [[Castleford Tigers]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60764/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-thursday |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Thursday |publisher=RFL |date=18 August 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} In the final round of the regular season, a 16–14 win over [[Wakefield Trinity]] in extra time was enough to secure third place in the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} In the opening round of the play-offs, Huddersfield faced sixth-placed [[Salford Red Devils]] but saw their season ended by a 28–0 loss.{{cite web|url= https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60894/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-september-10 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} September 10 |publisher=RFL |date=10 September 2022 |access-date=15
